title: Listening - Part 1
description: You are going to read a magazine article about an intellectual process known as critical thinking. For questions 31 – 36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text. Mark your answers on the separate answer sheet.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing an immersive art exhibition they have just visited.
extracts.audio_script: Man: So, what did you think of the 'Neon Dreams' exhibit? I found the sensory overload a bit much at first. It felt like every wall was shouting at me. || Woman: I get that. It wasn't exactly subtle, was it? But honestly, I think [1]the way they integrated the ambient soundscapes with the pulsating visuals really elevated it beyond just being a typical 'selfie gallery'[/1]. || Man: True, though I felt the ticket price was a bit steep for a forty-minute experience. I expected more historical context about the artists behind the movement. || Woman: I see where you're coming from, but I think the point was the sensory experience itself, not a history lesson. [2]I was pleasantly surprised that it didn't feel as crowded as the online reviews suggested[/2], which made it much more intimate than I’d hoped. || Man: Fair point. At least we didn't have to queue for an hour in the rain.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So, what did you think of the 'Neon Dreams' exhibit? I found the sensory overload a bit much at first. It felt like every wall was shouting at me.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I get that. It wasn't exactly subtle, was it? But honestly, I think the way they integrated the ambient soundscapes with the pulsating visuals really elevated it beyond just being a typical 'selfie gallery'.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: True, though I felt the ticket price was a bit steep for a forty-minute experience. I expected more historical context about the artists behind the movement.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I see where you're coming from, but I think the point was the sensory experience itself, not a history lesson. I was pleasantly surprised that it didn't feel as crowded as the online reviews suggested, which made it much more intimate than I’d hoped.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Fair point. At least we didn't have to queue for an hour in the rain.

questions.question: What did the woman like most about the exhibition?

options.B: the fusion of different artistic media
options.C: the opportunity to take unique photographs

questions.explanation: B is supported by [1] because she mentions the integration of soundscapes and visuals as a key highlight.

questions.question: Regarding the venue, the woman was impressed by

options.A: the efficiency of the entry system.
options.B: the spaciousness of the display rooms.
options.C: the limited number of other visitors.

questions.explanation: C is correct as she notes [2] it 'didn't feel as crowded' as expected, making it more intimate.

extracts.context: You hear two university students talking about a guest lecture they attended on deep-sea biology.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: That lecture on deep-sea bioluminescence was fascinating, don't you think? Professor Aris certainly knows his stuff and has a real passion for the subject. || Man: He’s brilliant, but I struggled to stay focused during the technical breakdown of the chemical reactions. It felt like he assumed we were all post-doctoral researchers. || Woman: [3]I actually appreciated that he didn't talk down to us[/3]. It’s refreshing to be genuinely challenged in a seminar rather than just hearing the same introductory material over and over again. It keeps us on our toes. || Man: I suppose. I did find the high-definition footage of the Anglerfish absolutely incredible, though. || Woman: Absolutely. [4]I’m just worried that I won’t be able to replicate his specific data analysis for our upcoming assignment[/4]—it looked incredibly complex and I missed some of the steps. || Man: Don't worry, we can head to the library archives together and figure it out.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That lecture on deep-sea bioluminescence was fascinating, don't you think? Professor Aris certainly knows his stuff and has a real passion for the subject.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: He’s brilliant, but I struggled to stay focused during the technical breakdown of the chemical reactions. It felt like he assumed we were all post-doctoral researchers.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I actually appreciated that he didn't talk down to us. It’s refreshing to be genuinely challenged in a seminar rather than just hearing the same introductory material over and over again. It keeps us on our toes.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I suppose. I did find the high-definition footage of the Anglerfish absolutely incredible, though.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Absolutely. I’m just worried that I won’t be able to replicate his specific data analysis for our upcoming assignment—it looked incredibly complex and I missed some of the steps.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Don't worry, we can head to the library archives together and figure it out.

questions.question: What was the woman's reaction to the complexity of the lecture?

options.A: She felt it was inappropriately targeted.
options.C: She found the chemical explanations tedious.

questions.explanation: B is supported by [3] where she says she 'appreciated that he didn't talk down to us' and found being 'genuinely challenged' refreshing.

questions.question: The woman expresses concern about her ability to

options.A: apply a particular methodology to her own work.
options.B: remember the specific details of the visual aids.
options.C: access the necessary research papers in the library.

questions.explanation: A is correct because in [4] she worries about her ability to 'replicate his specific data analysis'.

extracts.context: You hear a couple discussing a hiking trip they have just completed.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: I’m still a bit sore from that climb yesterday, but the panoramic view from the summit was worth every single blister. It was breathtaking. || Man: It was stunning. I’m glad we took the northern trail, even if it was considerably steeper. The other route looked far too busy with groups of tourists. || Woman: Definitely. [5]What struck me most, though, was how poorly some of the other hikers we passed were equipped[/5] for such an environment. I saw one group in flip-flops! || Man: People really underestimate how quickly the weather changes up there. I’m just glad we brought the extra layers and waterproofs. || Woman: Me too. [6]I’m already thinking about where we should head next month, maybe somewhere coastal for a change of pace? Something involving more sand and less incline.[/6] || Man: That sounds like a wonderful plan. As long as it’s a bit flatter than yesterday!

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’m still a bit sore from that climb yesterday, but the panoramic view from the summit was worth every single blister. It was breathtaking.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It was stunning. I’m glad we took the northern trail, even if it was considerably steeper. The other route looked far too busy with groups of tourists.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Definitely. What struck me most, though, was how poorly some of the other hikers we passed were equipped for such an environment. I saw one group in flip-flops!

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: People really underestimate how quickly the weather changes up there. I’m just glad we brought the extra layers and waterproofs.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Me too. I’m already thinking about where we should head next month, maybe somewhere coastal for a change of pace? Something involving more sand and less incline.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That sounds like a wonderful plan. As long as it’s a bit flatter than yesterday!

questions.question: What did the woman find particularly notable during the hike?

options.A: the sudden changes in the weather conditions
options.B: the irresponsible behavior of some people
options.C: the physical difficulty of the northern trail

questions.explanation: B is supported by [5] where she comments on hikers being 'poorly equipped' for the environment.

questions.question: What does the woman suggest for their next trip?

options.A: returning to the same area to try a different route
options.B: taking a break from physical activity altogether
options.C: choosing a location with a different topography

questions.explanation: C is correct as she suggests [6] a coastal trip with 'less incline' for a change of pace.
